volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 5.2.910b - Section 5.2.945)

sections.label: Section 5.2.910b
sections.translation: Will some one run to fetch the undertakers [*] ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.911
sections.translation: Health to you , mother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911b
sections.translation: Health indeed after this fashion !

sections.label: Section 5.2.911c
sections.translation: Demaenetus is dead . ’ Tis time to betake myself hence : this battle bravely waxes hot . I ’ ll be off to Diabolus . I ’ ll tell him his orders are performed as he desired them ; and in the meantime I ’ ll persuade him that we should take a meal , while these people are squabbling .

sections.label: Section 5.2.915
sections.original: poste demum huc cras adducam ad lenam , ut viginti minas
sections.translation: Afterwards , in fine , I ’ ll bring him here to-morrow , to the procuress , that he may give her the twenty mince , that , in his turn , he in his passion may be enabled to obtain this damsel . I hope that Argyrippus will be able to be prevailed upon to allow him to pass each alternate night with him in her company ; for , unless I obtain that , I ’ ve lost my patron [*] ; so great is the passion of the man by reason of his love .

sections.label: Section 5.2.920
sections.original: Quid tibi hunc receptio ad te est meum virum ?
sections.translation: What business have you to give a retreat here in your house to my husband ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.920b
sections.translation: Troth , he really will this day be the death of wretched me , through sheer disgust .

sections.label: Section 5.2.921b
sections.translation: Rise , wencher , be off home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.922
sections.translation: I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 5.2.922b
sections.original: Immo es , ne nega , omnium unus pol nequissimus .
sections.translation: Yes , you are [*] ; don ’ t , i ’ faith , gainsay it , you most vile of all men . Why , the cuckoo ’ s still on his nest [*] . Rise , wencher , be off home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.924
sections.translation: Woe to me !

sections.label: Section 5.2.924b
sections.original: Vera hariolare . surge , amator , i domum .
sections.translation: You prophesy correctly . Rise , wencher , be off home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.925
sections.translation: Step a little this way then .

sections.label: Section 5.2.925b
sections.translation: Rise , wencher , be off home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.926
sections.translation: Now , prithee , wife —

sections.label: Section 5.2.926b
sections.original: Nunc uxorem me esse meministi tuam ?
sections.translation: Do you remember now that I ’ m your wife ? It was but just now , when you were heaping abuse upon me , that I wasn ’ t your wife .

sections.label: Section 5.2.928
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly ruined .

sections.label: Section 5.2.928b
sections.original: Quid tandem ? anima fetetne uxoris tuae ?
sections.translation: Why , pray ? Does the breath of your wife smell strong ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.929
sections.translation: It smells of myrrh .

sections.label: Section 5.2.929b
sections.original: Iam subrupuisti pallam , quam scorto dares ?
sections.translation: Have you filched my mantle then to be giving to your harlot ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.930
sections.original: Ecastor qui subrupturum pallam promisit tibi .
sections.translation: By the powers — what , did he promise that he would filch your mantle ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.931
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.931b
sections.translation: I was going to dissuade him , mother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.931c
sections.translation: A pretty son ! ( To DEMAENETUS .) Is it proper for a father to teach these morals to his children ? Are you ashamed of nothing ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.933b
sections.original: Pol , si aliud nil sit , tui me , uxor , pudet .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if there ’ s nothing else , I ’ m ashamed of you , wife .

sections.label: Section 5.2.934
sections.original: Cano capite te cuculum uxor ex lustris rapit .
sections.translation: With your hoary head , your wife is dragging you , you cuckoo , from dens of infamy .

sections.label: Section 5.2.935
sections.original: Non licet manere ( cena coquitur ) dum cenem modo ?
sections.translation: The dinner ’ s cooking ; mayn ’ t I stop , only to take my dinner ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.936
sections.original: Ecastor cenabis hodie , ut dignus es , magnum malum .
sections.translation: Faith , you ’ ll dine to-day on a heavy mishap , as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 5.2.937
sections.original: Male cubandum est : iudicatum me uxor abducit domum .
sections.translation: I shall repose but uncomfortably ; my wife is taking me home condemned .

sections.label: Section 5.2.938
sections.original: Dicebam , pater , tibi , ne matri consuleres male .
sections.translation: I told you , father , not to devise ill against my mother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.939
sections.translation: Do remember about the mantle [*] , there ’ s a dear .

sections.label: Section 5.2.939b
sections.translation: Won ’ t you order her to go away from here ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.940
sections.translation: Be off home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.940b
sections.original: Da savium etiam prius quam abis .
sections.translation: Do give me a kiss , at least , before you go .

sections.label: Section 5.2.940c
sections.translation: Go hang yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.941
sections.original: Immo intro potius . sequere hac me , mí anime .
sections.translation: No , I ’ ll go in , in preference . ( To ARGYRIPPUS .) Follow me this way , my life .

sections.label: Section 5.2.941b
sections.translation: Yes , I follow .

sections.label: Section 5.2.942
sections.original: Hic senex si quid clam uxorem suo animo fecit volup ,
sections.translation: If this old fellow , unknown to his wife , has been in any way indulging his own inclinations , he has been doing nothing new or wonderful , or otherwise than others are in the habit of doing . No one is there of a disposition so severe , or of a temper so firm ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.945
sections.original: quin ubi quicque occasionis sit sibi faciat bene .
sections.translation: but that he will enjoy himself when he has any opportunity . Now if you wish to interpose in behalf of this old man , so that he be not punished , we think that it can be brought about if you give us loud applause .
